key_insight: RNC-LM improves Levenberg-Marquardt by using Riemann normal coordinates to add higher-order geometric corrections, making finite optimization steps more consistent with the underlying manifold geometry.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more robust solver for curved, ill-conditioned nonlinear least-squares problems in applications such as PINNs, regression, and large-scale parameter fitting.
method: Reformulates the geodesic equation in Riemann normal coordinates to extend geodesic acceleration beyond second order.
Constructs finite-step updates that progressively remove parameter-effect curvature in a moving tangent frame.
Uses a line search along the RNC curve to control step length while staying close to standard LM cost behavior.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports better convergence and robustness on classical nonlinear least-squares benchmarks, a reaction-diffusion PINN failure case with relative L2 error around 1e-3, and a 34-fold speedup on a large-scale potential-energy-surface fitting task.
watch_item: The added geometric corrections and line-search machinery may increase implementation complexity and require careful handling to preserve the computational efficiency of LM.
paper_title: Higher-Order Geometric Updates for Levenberg-Marquardt Method via Riemann Normal Coordinates
